up.method: NO2- consumption measured colourimetrically (Shinn/Doane-Horwath); apparent whole-cell Km(app)=54 uM NO2- (s.e.=7) at 25C pH6 by O2 microrespirometry; per-cell qS not reported in mass units

sec.method: NO3- produced stoichiometrically (1:1) from NO2- consumed (Fig S1); rate not reported in mass units

growth_records.cite: Bachtsevani, Eleftheria et al. Acidotolerant soil nitrite oxidizer “Candidatus Nitrobacter laanbroekii” NHB1 alleviates constraints on growth of acidophilic soil ammonia oxidizers. ISME Communications (2025). DOI:10.1093/ismeco/ycaf244. [PMC12815253]
growth_records.snippet: Growth of NHB1 occurred with initial NO2- concentrations up to 5 mM (inhibited at ~10 mM) and temperatures ranging 10C-30C, with a maximum specific growth rate (mumax) of 1.17 d-1 (s.e. = 0.05) at 25C and 0.5 mM NO2-
growth_records.notes: Optimal condition (25C, pH6.0, 500 uM NO2-). mumax reported 1.17 d-1; converted to h-1: 1.17/24 = 0.04875 h-1. Doubling time td = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.04875 = 14.22 h. mumax calculated by authors from exponential-phase NO2- consumption after correcting abiotic degradation. Km(app) 54 uM NO2- (whole-cell, O2 microrespirometry). Substrate=nitrite, product=nitrate (chemolithoautotroph). No mmol/gDW/h rates reported.

up.method: NO2- consumption; rate not reported in mass units

growth_records.cite: Bachtsevani, Eleftheria et al. Acidotolerant soil nitrite oxidizer “Candidatus Nitrobacter laanbroekii” NHB1 alleviates constraints on growth of acidophilic soil ammonia oxidizers. ISME Communications (2025). DOI:10.1093/ismeco/ycaf244. [PMC12815253]
growth_records.snippet: reduction of initial NO2- concentration to 100 uM decreased optimal growth pH to 5.5 [mumax 0.93 d-1 (s.e. = 0.01)]
growth_records.notes: At 100 uM initial NO2-, optimal pH shifted to 5.5, mumax=0.93 d-1. Converted: 0.93/24=0.03875 h-1; td=ln2/0.03875=17.89 h. 25C throughout the nitrite/pH series.

up.method: NO2- consumption; rate not reported in mass units

growth_records.cite: Bachtsevani, Eleftheria et al. Acidotolerant soil nitrite oxidizer “Candidatus Nitrobacter laanbroekii” NHB1 alleviates constraints on growth of acidophilic soil ammonia oxidizers. ISME Communications (2025). DOI:10.1093/ismeco/ycaf244. [PMC12815253]
growth_records.snippet: further reductions in pH growth optimum to 5.0 at 50 and 20 uM [mumax 0.77 d-1 (s.e. = 0.03) and 0.70 d-1 (s.e. = 0.01), respectively]
growth_records.notes: 50 uM initial NO2-, optimal pH 5.0, mumax=0.77 d-1. Converted: 0.77/24=0.03208 h-1; td=ln2/0.03208=21.6 h.

up.method: NO2- consumption; rate not reported in mass units

growth_records.cite: Bachtsevani, Eleftheria et al. Acidotolerant soil nitrite oxidizer “Candidatus Nitrobacter laanbroekii” NHB1 alleviates constraints on growth of acidophilic soil ammonia oxidizers. ISME Communications (2025). DOI:10.1093/ismeco/ycaf244. [PMC12815253]
growth_records.snippet: pH growth optimum to 5.0 at 50 and 20 uM [mumax 0.77 d-1 (s.e. = 0.03) and 0.70 d-1 (s.e. = 0.01), respectively]
growth_records.notes: 20 uM initial NO2-, optimal pH 5.0, mumax=0.70 d-1. Converted: 0.70/24=0.02917 h-1; td=ln2/0.02917=23.77 h.

up.method: NO2- consumption; rate not reported in mass units

growth_records.cite: Bachtsevani, Eleftheria et al. Acidotolerant soil nitrite oxidizer “Candidatus Nitrobacter laanbroekii” NHB1 alleviates constraints on growth of acidophilic soil ammonia oxidizers. ISME Communications (2025). DOI:10.1093/ismeco/ycaf244. [PMC12815253]
growth_records.snippet: At the lowest NO2- concentration, the limit for growth was extended to pH 3.5 [mumax 0.26 d-1 (s.e. = 0.01)]
growth_records.notes: Lowest pH (3.5) growth limit at low NO2- (~20 uM), mumax=0.26 d-1. Converted: 0.26/24=0.01083 h-1; td=ln2/0.01083=63.98 h. Demonstrates acidotolerant growth extension when nitrite kept low.
